Boundary element model (BEM)¶

This module may be employed to calcualte the boundary element model for EEG/MEG source reconstruction.

source_reconstruction.bem_model.read_skull_and_skin_models(subject_path, subj_name)¶

Reads skull and skin models extracted via freesurfer’s watershed algorithm.

Parameters:

  • subject_path – Subject’s freesurfer path.

  • subj_name – Subject name.

Returns:

Vertices and faces of the inner skull, outer skull, and skin models.

source_reconstruction.bem_model.calc_skull_and_skin_models(subject_path, subject_id, preflood_height=25, overwrite=False)¶

Employs freesufers watershed algorithm to calculate skull and skin models.

Parameters:

  • subject_path – Subjects freesurfer path.

  • subject_id – Subject name.

  • preflood_height – Freesurfer parameter. May need adjusting if segmentation doesn’t work properly.

  • overwrite – Flag to overwrite if files are already present. Defaults to False.

source_reconstruction.bem_model.calc_bem_model_linear_basis(ws_in_skull_vert, ws_in_skull_faces, tgt_icosahedron_level=4)¶

Calcuates the BEM linear basis coefficients from the collocation method.

Parameters:

  • ws_in_skull_vert – Inner skull vertices.

  • ws_in_skull_faces – Inner skull faces.

  • tgt_icosahedron_level – Order of the icosahedron employed herein.

Returns:

Scaled and filtered MRI vertices/faces, face area, face normals, and bem_solution.

source_reconstruction.bem_model.plot_skull_and_skin_models(ws_in_skull_vert, ws_in_skull_faces, ws_out_skull_vert, ws_out_skull_faces, ws_out_skin_vect, ws_out_skin_faces, subject_path)¶

Plot skull and skin models for visual confirmation of proper alignment.

Parameters:

  • ws_in_skull_vert – Vertices of the inner skull model.

  • ws_in_skull_faces – Faces of the inner skull model.

  • ws_out_skull_vert – Vertices of the outer skull model.

  • ws_out_skull_faces – Faces of the outer skull model.

  • ws_out_skin_vert – Vertices of the skin model.

  • ws_out_skin_faces – Faces of the skin model.

  • subject_path – Subject’s freesurfer path.

source_reconstruction.bem_model._load_and_orient_t1(subject_path)¶

Loads and orients an MRI scan.

Parameters:

subject_path – Subject’s freesurfer path.

Returns:

Data and orientation of the scan.

source_reconstruction.bem_model._plot_skull_and_skin_subplots(data, axes, surfaces)¶

Adds splices from the MRI scan onto the plot.

Parameters:

  • data – The transformed data for plotting.

  • axes – Axes do plot data onto.

  • surfaces – Surfaces (inner skull, skin, and/or outer skull models.

source_reconstruction.bem_model._find_non_diag_omega(faces, vert, double_faces_area, faces_normal)¶

Calculates the non-diagonal elements of omega, see function calc_bem_model.

Parameters:

  • faces – Individual faces.

  • vert – Individual vertices.

  • double_faces_area – Precomputed double face area.

  • faces_normal – Normales of each face.

Returns:

Thre respective omega elements.

source_reconstruction.bem_model._find_diag_omega(omega, faces)¶

Calculates the diagonal elements of omega as these cannot be calculated as the non-diagonal ones due to the auto solid angle problem.

See “Error Analysis of a New Galerkin Method to Solve the Forward Problem in MEG and EEG Using the Boundary Element Method” by Satu Tissari, Jussi Rahola for reference. See function calc_bem_model for a more general description.

Parameters:

  • omega – The omega matrix with non-diagonal elements populated.

  • faces – Respective faces.

Returns:

The respective omega elements.

source_reconstruction.bem_model._calc_bem_fields(vortex, rmags, cosmags)¶

Calculates the magnetic field at a vortex from all MEG sensors.

Parameters:

  • vortex – Position of the vortex.

  • rmags – 3D position of the MEG coils.

  • cosmags – MEG coil direction.

Returns:

The magnetic field of all MEG sensors at a vortex.
